name: epsgeo
documentation: Variable used in the approximate calculations.

name: addMargins
documentation: Add margins to 'pic' with respect to the current bounding box of 'pic'. If 'rigid' is false, margins are added iff an infinite curve will be prolonged on the margin. If 'allObject' is false, fixed - size objects (such as labels and arrowheads) will be ignored.

name: binomial
documentation: Return n!/((n - k)!*k!)

name: rf
documentation: Computes Carlson's elliptic integral of the first kind. x, y, and z must be non negative, and at most one can be zero.

name: rd
documentation: Computes Carlson's elliptic integral of the second kind. x and y must be positive, and at most one can be zero. z must be non negative.

name: elle
documentation: Legendre elliptic integral of the 2nd kind, evaluated using Carlson's functions RD and RF. The argument ranges are -infinity < phi < +infinity, 0 <= k * sin(phi) <= 1.

name: intersectionpoints
documentation: Intersection points with the line (AB) and the quadric curve a * x^2 + b * x * y + c * y^2 + d * x + f * y + g = 0 given in the default coordinate system

name: intersectionpoints
documentation: Return the intersection points of the line AB with the conic whose an equation is equation[0] * x^2 + equation[1] * x * y + equation[2] * y^2 + equation[3] * x + equation[4] * y + equation[5] = 0
Function type to convert pair in an other coordinate system.
Function type to calculate modulus of pair.
Function type to calculate dot product.
Function type to calculate the coordinates from the polar coordinates.

name: coordsys
documentation: This structure represents a coordinate system in the plane.*/ /**/ restricted convert relativetodefault = new pair(pair m){return m;};/*Convert a pair given relatively to this coordinate system to the pair relatively to the default coordinate system.

name: defaulttorelativet
documentation: Convert a pair given relatively to the default coordinate system to the pair relatively to this coordinate system.

name: dot
documentation: Return the dot product of this coordinate system.

name: abs
documentation: Return the modulus of a pair in this coordinate system.

name: polar
documentation: Polar coordinates routine of this coordinate system.

name: O,i,j
documentation: Origin and units vector.

name: init
documentation: The default constructor of the coordinate system.

name: cartesiansystem
documentation: Return the Cartesian coordinate system (O, i, j).

name: show
documentation: Draw the components (O, i, j, x - axis, y - axis) of 'R'.

name: defaultcoordsys
documentation: One can always refer to the default coordinate system using this constant.

name: currentcoordsys
documentation: The coordinate system used by default.

name: point
documentation: This structure replaces the pair to embed its coordinate system. For example, if 'P = point(cartesiansystem((1, 2), i, j), (0, 0))', P is equal to the pair (1, 2).*/ /**/ coordsys coordsys;/*The coordinate system of this point.*/ restricted pair coordinates;/*The coordinates of this point relatively to the coordinate system 'coordsys'.*/ restricted real x, y;/*The xpart and the ypart of 'coordinates'.

name: init
documentation: Used to cast mass<->point.

name: point
documentation: Return the point which has the coodinates 'p' in the coordinate system 'R' and the mass 'm'.

name: point
documentation: Return the point which has the coodinates 'p' in the current coordinate system and the mass 'm'.

name: point
documentation: Return the point of 'R' which has the coordinates of 'M' and the mass 'm'. Do not confuse this routine with the further routine 'changecoordsys'.

name: changecoordsys
documentation: Return the point 'M' in the coordinate system 'coordsys'. In other words, the returned point marks the same plot as 'M' does.

name: pair coordinates
documentation: Return the coordinates of 'M' in its coordinate system.

name: bool samecoordsys
documentation: Return true iff all the points have the same coordinate system. If 'warn' is true and the coordinate systems are different, a warning is sent.

name: standardizecoordsys
documentation: Return the points with the same coordinate system 'R'. If 'warn' is true and the coordinate systems are different, a warning is sent.

name: locate
documentation: Return the coordinates of 'P' in the default coordinate system.

name: locate
documentation: Return the point in the current coordinate system 'currentcoordsys'.

name: abs
documentation: Return the modulus |m| in the coordinate system 'R'.

name: abs
documentation: Return the modulus |M| in its coordinate system.

name: length
documentation: Return the modulus |M| in its coordinate system (same as 'abs').

name: conj
documentation: Conjugate.

name: degrees
documentation: Return the angle of M (in degrees) relatively to 'R'.

name: angle
documentation: Return the angle of M (in radians) relatively to 'R'.

name: finite
documentation: Avoid to compute 'finite((pair)(infinite_point))'.

name: dot
documentation: Return the dot product in the coordinate system of 'A'.

name: dot
documentation: Return the dot product in the default coordinate system. dot(explicit pair, point) is also defined.

name: rotateO
documentation: Rotation around the origin of the current coordinate system.

name: projection
documentation: Return the orthogonal projection on the line (AB).

name: projection
documentation: Return the (CD) parallel projection on (AB). If 'safe = true' and (AB)//(CD) return the identity. If 'safe = false' and (AB)//(CD) return an infinity scaling.

name: scale
documentation: Homothety.

name: xscale
documentation: xscale from 'M' relatively to the x - axis of the coordinate system of 'M'.

name: yscale
documentation: yscale from 'M' relatively to the y - axis of the coordinate system of 'M'.

name: scale
documentation: <url href = "http://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Affinit%C3%A9_%28math%C3%A9matiques%29"/> (help me for English translation...) If 'safe = true' and (AB)//(CD) return the identity. If 'safe = false' and (AB)//(CD) return a infinity scaling.

name: scaleO
documentation: Homothety from the origin of the current coordinate system.

name: xscaleO
documentation: xscale from the origin and relatively to the current coordinate system.

name: yscaleO
documentation: yscale from the origin and relatively to the current coordinate system.

name: vector
documentation: Like a point but casting to pair, adding etc does not take account of the origin of the coordinate system.*/ point v;/*Coordinates as a point (embed coordinate system and pair).

name: vector
documentation: Return the vector of 'R' which has the coordinates 'v'.

name: vector
documentation: Return the vector OM, where O is the origin of the coordinate system of 'M'. Useful to write 'vector(P - M);' instead of '(vector)(P - M)'.

name: point
documentation: Return the point M so that OM = u, where O is the origin of the coordinate system of 'u'.

name: locate
documentation: Return the coordinates of 'v' in the default coordinate system (like casting vector to pair).

name: show
documentation: Draw the vector v (from the origin of its coordinate system).

name: changecoordsys
documentation: Return the vector 'v' relatively to coordinate system 'R'.

name: collinear
documentation: Return 'true' iff the vectors 'u' and 'v' are collinear.

name: unit
documentation: Return the unit vector according to the modulus of its coordinate system.

name: unit
documentation: Return the unit vector according to the modulus of its coordinate system.

name: degrees
documentation: Return the angle of 'v' (in degrees) relatively to 'R'.

name: angle
documentation: Return the angle of 'v' (in radians) relatively to 'R'.

name: conj
documentation: Conjugate.

name: rotate
documentation: A rotation in the direction 'dir' limited to [-90, 90] This is useful for rotating text along a line in the direction dir. rotate(explicit point dir) is also defined.

name: origin
documentation: The origin of the current coordinate system.

name: origin
documentation: Return the origin of the coordinate system 'R'.

name: linemargin
documentation: Margin used to draw lines.

name: linemargin
documentation: Return the margin used to draw lines.

name: addpenline
documentation: Add this property to the drawing pen of "finish" lines.

name: addpenarc
documentation: Add this property to the drawing pen of arcs.

name: defaultmassformat
documentation: Format used to construct the default label of masses.

name: sgnd
documentation: Return the -1 if x < 0, 1 if x >= 0.

name: defined
documentation: Return true iff the coordinates of 'P' are finite.

name: onpath
documentation: Return true iff 'M' is on the path drawn with the pen 'p' in 'pic'.

name: sameside
documentation: Return 'true' iff 'M' and 'N' are same side of the point 'O'.

name: between
documentation: Return 'true' iff 'O' is between 'M' and 'N'.

name: clipdraw
documentation: Draw the path 'g' on 'pic' clipped to the bounding box of 'pic'.

name: distance
documentation: Draw arrow between A and B (from FAQ).

name: perpfactor
documentation: Factor for drawing perpendicular symbol.

name: perpendicularmark
documentation: Draw a perpendicular symbol at z aligned in the direction align relative to the path z--z + dir. dir(45 + n * 90), where n in N*, are common values for 'align'.

name: perpendicularmark
documentation: Draw a perpendicular symbol at z aligned in the direction align relative to the path z--z + dir. dir(45 + n * 90), where n in N, are common values for 'align'.

name: perpendicularmark
documentation: Draw a perpendicular symbol at z aligned in the direction align relative to the path z--z + dir(g, 0). dir(45 + n * 90), where n in N, are common values for 'align'.

name: perpendicularmark
documentation: Draw a perpendicular symbol at z aligned in the direction align relative to the path z--z + dir(g, 0). dir(45 + n * 90), where n in N, are common values for 'align'.

name: markrightangle
documentation: Mark the angle AOB with a perpendicular symbol.

name: simeq
documentation: Return true iff abs(A - B) < fuzz. This routine is used internally to know if two points are equal, in particular by the operator == in 'point == point'.

name: attract
documentation: Return the nearest point (A PAIR) of 'm' which is on the path g. 'fuzz' is the argument 'fuzz' of 'intersect'.

name: attract
documentation: Return the nearest point (A POINT) of 'M' which is on the path g. 'fuzz' is the argument 'fuzz' of 'intersect'.

name: line
documentation: This structure provides the objects line, semi - line and segment oriented from A to B. All the calculus with this structure will be as exact as Asymptote can do. For a full precision, you must not cast 'line' to 'path' excepted for drawing routines.*/ /**/ restricted point A,B;/*Two line's points with same coordinate system.*/ bool extendA,extendB;/*If true,extend 'l' in direction of A (resp. B).*/ restricted vector u,v;/*u = unit(AB) = direction vector,v = normal vector.*/ restricted real a,b,c;/*Coefficients of the equation ax + by + c = 0 in the coordinate system of 'A'.*/ restricted real slope, origin;/*Slope and ordinate at the origin.

name: copy
documentation: Copy a line in a new instance.

name: init
documentation: Initialize line. If 'extendA' is true, the "line" is infinite in the direction of A.

name: line
documentation: Return the line passing through 'A' and 'B'. If 'extendA' is true, the "line" is infinite in the direction of A. A "line" can be half-line or segment.

name: segment
documentation: .*/ restricted point A, B;// Extremity. restricted vector u, v;// u = direction vector, v = normal vector. restricted real a, b, c;// Coefficients of the equation ax + by + c = 0 restricted real slope, origin; segment copy() { segment s = new segment; s.A = A; s.B = B; s.a = a; s.b = b; s.c = c; s.slope = slope; s.origin = origin; s.u = u; s.v = v; return s; } void init(point A, point B) { line l; l.init(A, B); this.A = l.A; this.B = l.B; this.a = l.a; this.b = l.b; this.c = l.c; this.slope = l.slope; this.origin = l.origin; this.u = l.u; this.v = l.v; } }/**/ /**/ segment segment(point A, point B) {/*Return the segment whose the extremities are A and B.

name: length
documentation: Return the length of 's'.

name: sameside
documentation: Return 'true' iff 'M' and 'N' are same side of the line (or on the line) 'l'.

name: line
documentation: Return the line passing through 's.A' and 's.B'.

name: segment
documentation: Return the segment whose extremities are 'l.A' and 'l.B'.

name: midpoint
documentation: Return the midpoint of 's'.

name: write
documentation: Write some informations about 'l'.

name: write
documentation: Write some informations about 's'.

name: coordsys
documentation: Return the coordinate system in which 'l' is defined.

name: reverse
documentation: Permute the points 'A' and 'B' of 'l' and so its orientation.

name: extend
documentation: Return the infinite line passing through 'l.A' and 'l.B'.

name: complementary
documentation: Return the complementary of a half-line with respect of the full line 'l'.

name: complementary
documentation: Return the two half-lines of origin 's.A' and 's.B' respectively.

name: Ox
documentation: Return the x-axis of 'R'.

name: Ox
documentation: the x-axis of the default coordinate system.

name: Oy
documentation: Return the y-axis of 'R'.

name: Oy
documentation: the y-axis of the default coordinate system.

name: line
documentation: Return the line passing through 'A' with an angle (in the coordinate system of A) 'a' in degrees. line(point, real) is also defined.

name: line
documentation: Return the line defined by slope and y-intercept relative to 'R'.

name: line
documentation: Retrun the line defined by equation relative to 'R'.

name: vline
documentation: Return a vertical line in 'R' passing through the origin of 'R'.

name: vline
documentation: The vertical line in the current coordinate system passing through the origin of this system.

name: hline
documentation: Return a horizontal line in 'R' passing through the origin of 'R'.

name: hline
documentation: The horizontal line in the current coordinate system passing through the origin of this system.

name: changecoordsys
documentation: Return the line 'l' in the coordinate system 'R'.

name: scale
documentation: Return the dilatation with respect to 'l1' in the direction of 'l2'.

name: reflect
documentation: Return the reflect about the line 'l'.

name: reflect
documentation: Return the reflect about the line 'l1' in the direction of 'l2'.

name: intersectionpoints
documentation: Return all points of intersection of the line 'l' with the path 'g'.

name: intersectionpoint
documentation: Return the point of intersection of line 'l1' with 'l2'. If 'l1' and 'l2' have an infinity or none point of intersection, this routine return (infinity, infinity).

name: parallel
documentation: Return the line parallel to 'l' passing through 'M'.

name: parallel
documentation: Return the line of direction 'dir' and passing through 'M'.

name: parallel
documentation: Return the line of direction 'dir' and passing through 'M'.

name: parallel
documentation: Return 'true' if 'l1' and 'l2' are (strictly ?) parallel.

name: concurrent
documentation: Returns true if all the lines 'l' are concurrent.

name: projection
documentation: Return the orthogonal projection on 'l'.

name: projection
documentation: Return the projection on (AB) in parallel of (CD). If 'safe = true' and (l1)//(l2) return the identity. If 'safe = false' and (l1)//(l2) return a infinity scaling.

name: vprojection
documentation: Return the projection on 'l' in parallel of N--S. If 'safe' is 'true' the projected point keeps the same place if 'l' is vertical.

name: hprojection
documentation: Return the projection on 'l' in parallel of E--W. If 'safe' is 'true' the projected point keeps the same place if 'l' is horizontal.

name: perpendicular
documentation: Return the perpendicular line of 'l' passing through 'M'.

name: perpendicular
documentation: Return the line passing through 'M' whose normal is \param{normal}.

name: perpendicular
documentation: Return the line passing through 'M' whose normal is \param{normal} (given in the currentcoordsys).

name: perpendicular
documentation: Return 'true' if 'l1' and 'l2' are perpendicular.

name: angle
documentation: Return the angle of the oriented line 'l', in radian, in the interval ]-pi, pi] and relatively to 'R'.

name: degrees
documentation: Returns the angle of the oriented line 'l' in degrees, in the interval [0, 360[ and relatively to 'R'.

name: sharpangle
documentation: Return the measure in radians of the sharp angle formed by 'l1' and 'l2'.

name: angle
documentation: Return the measure in radians of oriented angle (l1.u, l2.u).

name: degrees
documentation: Return the measure in degrees of the angle formed by the oriented lines 'l1' and 'l2'.

name: sharpdegrees
documentation: Return the measure in degrees of the sharp angle formed by 'l1' and 'l2'.

name: bisector
documentation: Return the bisector of the angle formed by 'l1' and 'l2' rotated by the angle 'angle' (in degrees) around intersection point of 'l1' with 'l2'. If 'sharp' is true (the default), this routine returns the bisector of the sharp angle. Note that the returned line inherit of coordinate system of 'l1'.

name: sector
documentation: Return the p-th nth-sector of the angle formed by the oriented line 'l1' and 'l2' rotated by the angle 'angle' (in degrees) around the intersection point of 'l1' with 'l2'. If 'sharp' is true (the default), this routine returns the bisector of the sharp angle. Note that the returned line inherit of coordinate system of 'l1'.

name: bisector
documentation: Return the bisector of the angle formed by the lines (AB) and (CD). <look href = "#bisector(line, line, real, bool)"/>.

name: bisector
documentation: Return the bisector of the segment line 's' rotated by 'angle' (in degrees) around the midpoint of 's'.

name: bisector
documentation: Return the bisector of the segment line [AB] rotated by 'angle' (in degrees) around the midpoint of [AB].

name: distance
documentation: Return the distance from 'M' to 'l'. distance(line, point) is also defined.

name: draw
documentation: Draw the line 'l' without altering the size of picture pic. The boolean parameters control the infinite section. The global variable 'linemargin' (default value is 0) allows to modify the bounding box in which the line must be drawn.

name: draw
documentation: Draw each lines with the corresponding pen.

name: draw
documentation: Draw each lines with the same pen 'p'.

name: show
documentation: Draw some informations of 'l'.

name: sameside
documentation: Return two points on 'l1' and 'l2' respectively. The first point is from the same side of M relatively to 'l2', the second point is from the same side of M relatively to 'l1'.

name: markangle
documentation: Mark the angle (l1, l2) aligned in the direction 'align' relative to 'l1'. Commune values for 'align' are dir(real).

name: markangle
documentation: Mark the angle (l1, l2) in the direction 'dir' given relatively to 'l1'.

name: markangle
documentation: Mark the oriented angle (l1, l2).

name: perpendicularmark
documentation: Draw a right angle at the intersection point of lines and aligned in the 'quarter' nth quarter of circle formed by 'l1.u' and 'l2.u'.

name: bqe
documentation: Bivariate Quadratic Equation.*/ /**/ real[] a;/*a[0] * x^2 + a[1] * x * y + a[2] * y^2 + a[3] * x + a[4] * y + a[5] = 0*/ coordsys coordsys;/**/ }/**/ /**/ bqe bqe(coordsys R = currentcoordsys, real a, real b, real c, real d, real e, real f) {/*Return the bivariate quadratic equation a[0] * x^2 + a[1] * x * y + a[2] * y^2 + a[3] * x + a[4] * y + a[5] = 0 relatively to the coordinate system R.

name: changecoordsys
documentation: Returns the bivariate quadratic equation relatively to 'R'.

name: bqe
documentation: Return the bqe of conic passing through the five points (if possible).

name: samecoordsys
documentation: Return true if all the bivariate quadratic equations have the same coordinate system.

name: realquarticroots
documentation: Return the real roots of the quartic equation ax^4 + b^x3 + cx^2 + dx = 0.

name: conic
documentation: */ real e, p, h;/*BE CAREFUL: h = distance(F, D) and p = h * e (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ellipse) While http://mathworld.wolfram.com/ takes p = distance(F,D).*/ point F;/*Focus.*/ line D;/*Directrix.*/ line[] l;/*Case of degenerated conic (not yet implemented !).

name: circle
documentation: All the calculus with this structure will be as exact as Asymptote can do. For a full precision, you must not cast 'circle' to 'path' excepted for drawing routines.*/ /**/ point C;/*Center*/ real r;/*Radius*/ line l;/*If the radius is infinite, this line is used instead of circle.

name: ellipse
documentation: Look at http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Ellipse.html*/ /**/ restricted point F1,F2,C;/*Foci and center.*/ restricted real a,b,c,e,p;/**/ restricted real angle;/*Value is degrees(F2 - F1).*/ restricted line D1,D2;/*Directrices.*/ line l;/*If one axis is infinite, this line is used instead of ellipse.

name: init
documentation: Ellipse given by foci and semimajor axis.

name: parabola

If unset the current picture bounding box is used instead.

name: init
documentation: Parabola given by focus and directrix.

name: hyperbola
documentation: Look at http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Hyperbola.html*/ restricted point F1,F2;/*Foci.*/ restricted point C,V1,V2;/*Center and vertices.*/ restricted real a,b,c,e,p;/**/ restricted real angle;/*Value is degrees(F2 - F1).*/ restricted line D1,D2,A1,A2;/*Directrices and asymptotes.*/ pair bmin, bmax; /*The (left, bottom) and (right, top) coordinates of region bounding box for drawing the hyperbola. If unset the current picture bounding box is used instead.

name: init
documentation: Hyperbola given by foci and semimajor axis.

name: conicnodesfactor
documentation: Factor for the node number of all conics.

name: circlenodesnumberfactor
documentation: Factor for the node number of circles.

name: circlenodesnumber
documentation: Return the number of nodes for drawing a circle of radius 'r'.

name: circlenodesnumber
documentation: Return the number of nodes to draw a circle arc.

name: ellispenodesnumberfactor
documentation: Factor for the node number of ellispe (non-circle).

name: ellipsenodesnumber
documentation: Return the number of nodes to draw a ellipse of axis 'a' and 'b'.

name: ellipsenodesnumber
documentation: Return the number of nodes to draw an ellipse arc.

name: parabolanodesnumberfactor
documentation: Factor for the number of nodes of parabolas.

name: parabolanodesnumber
documentation: Return the number of nodes for drawing a parabola.

name: hyperbolanodesnumberfactor
documentation: Factor for the number of nodes of hyperbolas.

name: hyperbolanodesnumber
documentation: Return the number of nodes for drawing an hyperbola.

name: coordsys
documentation: Return the coordinate system of 'co'.

name: changecoordsys
documentation: Change the coordinate system of 'co' to 'R'
Routine type used to draw conics from 'angle1' to 'angle2'

name: arcfromfocus
documentation: Return the path of the conic section 'co' from angle1 to angle2 in degrees, drawing in the given direction, with n nodes.

name: currentpolarconicroutine
documentation: Default routine used to cast conic section to path.

name: angpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'co' whose the angular (in degrees) coordinate is 'angle' (mesured from the focus of 'co', relatively to its 'natural coordinate system').

name: coordsys
documentation: Return the coordinate system of 'el'.

name: canonicalcartesiansystem
documentation: Return the canonical cartesian system of the ellipse 'el'.

name: canonicalcartesiansystem
documentation: Return the canonical cartesian system of a parabola, so that Origin = vertex of 'p' and directrix: x = -a.

name: canonicalcartesiansystem
documentation: Return the canonical cartesian system of an hyperbola.

name: ellipse
documentation: Return the ellipse whose the foci are 'F1' and 'F2' and the semimajor axis is 'a'.

name: byfoci,byvertices
documentation: Constants useful for the routine 'hyperbola(point P1, point P2, real ae, bool byfoci = byfoci)'

name: hyperbola
documentation: if 'byfoci = true': return the hyperbola whose the foci are 'P1' and 'P2' and the semimajor axis is 'ae'. else return the hyperbola whose vertexes are 'P1' and 'P2' with eccentricity 'ae'.

name: ellipse
documentation: Return the ellipse passing through 'M' whose the foci are 'F1' and 'F2'.

name: ellipse
documentation: Return the ellipse centered at 'C' with semimajor axis 'a' along C--C + dir(angle), semiminor axis 'b' along the perpendicular.

name: ellipse
documentation: Return the ellipse a[0] * x^2 + a[1] * xy + a[2] * y^2 + a[3] * x + a[4] * y + a[5] = 0 given in the coordinate system of 'bqe' with a[i] = bque.a[i]. <url href = "http://mathworld.wolfram.com/QuadraticCurve.html"/> <url href = "http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Ellipse.html"/>.

name: ellipse
documentation: Return the ellipse passing through the five points (if possible)

name: inside
documentation: Return 'true' iff 'M' is inside 'el'.

name: inside
documentation: Return 'true' if 'M' is inside 'p'.

name: parabola
documentation: Return the parabola whose focus is 'F' and directrix is 'l'.

name: parabola
documentation: Return the parabola whose focus is 'F' and vertex is 'vertex'.

name: parabola
documentation: Return the parabola whose focus is F, latus rectum is 4a and the angle of the axis of symmetry (in the coordinate system of F) is 'angle'.

name: isparabola
documentation: Return true iff 'bqe' is the equation of a parabola.

name: parabola
documentation: Return the parabola a[0]x^2 + a[1]xy + a[2]y^2 + a[3]x + a[4]y + a[5]] = 0 (a[n] means bqe.a[n]). <url href = "http://mathworld.wolfram.com/QuadraticCurve.html"/> <url href = "http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Parabola.html"/>

name: parabola
documentation: Return the parabola passing through the three points with its directix parallel to the line 'l'.

name: parabola
documentation: Return the parabola passing through the five points.

name: hyperbola
documentation: Return the hyperbola passing through 'M' whose the foci are 'F1' and 'F2'.

name: hyperbola
documentation: Return the hyperbola centered at 'C' with semimajor axis 'a' along C--C + dir(angle), semiminor axis 'b' along the perpendicular.

name: hyperbola
documentation: Return the hyperbola a[0]x^2 + a[1]xy + a[2]y^2 + a[3]x + a[4]y + a[5]] = 0 (a[n] means bqe.a[n]). <url href = "http://mathworld.wolfram.com/QuadraticCurve.html"/> <url href = "http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Hyperbola.html"/>

name: hyperbola
documentation: Return the hyperbola passing through the five points (if possible).

name: conj
documentation: Conjugate.

name: circle
documentation: Circle given by center and radius.

name: circle
documentation: Return the circle of diameter AB.

name: circle
documentation: Return the circle of diameter 's'.

name: circumcenter
documentation: Return the circumcenter of triangle ABC.

name: circle
documentation: Return the circumcircle of the triangle ABC.

name: circumcircle
documentation: Return the circumcircle of the triangle ABC.

name: arcfromcenter
documentation: Return the path of the ellipse 'el' from angle1 to angle2 in degrees, drawing in the given direction, with n nodes. The angles are mesured relatively to the axis (C,x-axis) where C is the center of the ellipse.

name: arcfromcenter
documentation: Return the path of the hyperbola 'h' from angle1 to angle2 in degrees, drawing in the given direction, with n nodes. The angles are mesured relatively to the axis (C, x-axis) where C is the center of the hyperbola.

name: arcfromcenter
documentation: Use arcfromcenter(ellipse, ...) or arcfromcenter(hyperbola, ...) depending of the eccentricity of 'co'.

name: equation
documentation: Return the coefficients of the equation of the ellipse in its coordinate system: bqe.a[0] * x^2 + bqe.a[1] * x * y + bqe.a[2] * y^2 + bqe.a[3] * x + bqe.a[4] * y + bqe.a[5] = 0. One can change the coordinate system of 'bqe' using the routine 'changecoordsys'.

name: equation
documentation: Return the coefficients of the equation of the parabola in its coordinate system. bqe.a[0] * x^2 + bqe.a[1] * x * y + bqe.a[2] * y^2 + bqe.a[3] * x + bqe.a[4] * y + bqe.a[5] = 0 One can change the coordinate system of 'bqe' using the routine 'changecoordsys'.

name: equation
documentation: Return the coefficients of the equation of the hyperbola in its coordinate system. bqe.a[0] * x^2 + bqe.a[1] * x * y + bqe.a[2] * y^2 + bqe.a[3] * x + bqe.a[4] * y + bqe.a[5] = 0 One can change the coordinate system of 'bqe' using the routine 'changecoordsys'.

name: bangles
documentation: Return the array {ma, Ma} where 'ma' and 'Ma' are respectively the smaller and the larger angles for which the parabola 'p' is included in the bounding box of the picture 'pic'.

name: bangles
documentation: Return the array {{ma1, Ma1}, {ma2, Ma2}} where 'maX' and 'MaX' are respectively the smaller and the bigger angles (from h.FX) for which the hyperbola 'h' is included in the bounding box of the picture 'pic'.

name: draw
documentation: Draw the ellipse 'el' if it is not degenerated else draw 'el.l'.*/ if(degenerate(el)) draw(pic, L, el.l, align, p, arrow, legend, marker); else draw(pic, L, (path)el, align, p, arrow, bar, margin, legend, marker); } void fill(picture pic = currentpicture, ellipse el, pen p = currentpen) { if (!degenerate(el)) fill(pic, (path)el, p); } void filldraw(picture pic = currentpicture, ellipse el, pen fillpen = currentpen, pen drawpen = currentpen) { fill(pic, el, fillpen); draw(pic, el, drawpen); } /**/ void draw(picture pic = currentpicture, Label L = "", parabola parabola, align align = NoAlign, pen p = currentpen, arrowbar arrow = None, arrowbar bar = None, margin margin = NoMargin, Label legend = "", marker marker = nomarker) {/*Draw the parabola 'p' on 'pic' without (if possible) altering the size of picture pic.

name: draw
documentation: Draw the hyperbola 'h' on 'pic' without (if possible) altering the size of the picture pic.

name: draw
documentation: Use one of the routine 'draw(ellipse, ...)', 'draw(parabola, ...)' or 'draw(hyperbola, ...)' depending of the value of eccentricity of 'co'.

name: conicnodesnumber
documentation: Return the number of node to draw a conic arc.

name: equation
documentation: Return the coefficients of the equation of conic section in its coordinate system: bqe.a[0] * x^2 + bqe.a[1] * x * y + bqe.a[2] * y^2 + bqe.a[3] * x + bqe.a[4] * y + bqe.a[5] = 0. One can change the coordinate system of 'bqe' using the routine 'changecoordsys'.

name: conictype
documentation: Returned values are "ellipse" or "parabola" or "hyperbola" depending of the conic section represented by 'bqe'.

name: conic
documentation: Return the conic passing through 'M1', 'M2', 'M3', 'M4' and 'M5' if the conic is not degenerated.

name: canonicalcartesiansystem
documentation: Return the canonical cartesian system of the conic 'co'.

name: canonical
documentation: Return the bivariate quadratic equation relative to the canonical coordinate system of the conic section represented by 'bqe'.

name: conic
documentation: Return the conic section represented by the bivariate quartic equation 'bqe'.

name: focusToCenter
documentation: Return the angle relatively to the center of 'el' for the angle 'a' given relatively to the focus of 'el'.

name: centerToFocus
documentation: Return the angle relatively to the focus of 'el' for the angle 'a' given relatively to the center of 'el'.

name: arclength
documentation: Return the length of the arc of the ellipse between 'angle1' and 'angle2'. 'angle1' and 'angle2' must be in the interval ]-360;+oo[ if polarconicroutine = fromFocus, ]-oo;+oo[ if polarconicroutine = fromCenter.

name: arclength
documentation: Return the arclength from 180 to 'angle' given from focus in the canonical coordinate system of 'p'.

name: arclength
documentation: Return the arclength from 'angle1' to 'angle2' given from focus in the canonical coordinate system of 'p'

name: arclength
documentation: Return the length of the arc of the parabola bounded to the bounding box of the current picture.

name: abscissa
documentation: Provide abscissa structure on a curve used in the routine-like 'point(object, abscissa)' where object can be 'line','segment','ellipse','circle','conic'...*/ real x;/*The abscissa value.*/ int system;/*0 = relativesystem; 1 = curvilinearsystem; 2 = angularsystem; 3 = nodesystem*/ polarconicroutine polarconicroutine = fromCenter;/*The routine used with angular system and two foci conic section. Possible values are 'formCenter' and 'formFocus'.

name: relabscissa

name: curabscissa

name: angabscissa

name: nodabscissa
documentation: Return an abscissa as time on the path.

name: point

documentation: Return the point of 'el' which has the abscissa 'l.x' according to the abscissa system 'l.system'.

documentation: Return the point of 'h' which has the abscissa 'l.x' according to the abscissa system 'l.system'.

name: point
documentation: Return the curvilinear abscissa of 'M' on the conic 'co'.

name: point
documentation: Return the point of 'l' which has the abscissa 'l.x' according to the abscissa system 'l.system'. Note that the origin is l.A, and point(l, relabscissa(x)) returns l.A + x.x * vector(l.B - l.A).

name: point
documentation: Return the point between node l.A and l.B (x <= 0 means l.A, x >=1 means l.B).

name: point
documentation: Return the point between node floor(x) and floor(x) + 1.

name: point
documentation: Return the point between node floor(x) and floor(x) + 1.

name: point
documentation: Return the point between node floor(x) and floor(x) + 1.

name: point
documentation: Return the point between node floor(x) and floor(x) + 1.

name: point
documentation: Return the point between node floor(x) and floor(x) + 1.

name: relpoint
documentation: Return the relative point of 'l' (0 means l.A, 1 means l.B, x means l.A + x * vector(l.B - l.A) ).

name: relpoint
documentation: Return the relative point of 'c' (0 means origin, 1 means end). Origin is c.center + c.r * (1, 0).

name: relpoint
documentation: Return the relative point of 'el' (0 means origin, 1 means end).

name: relpoint
documentation: Return the relative point of the path of the parabola bounded by the bounding box of the current picture. 0 means origin, 1 means end, where the origin is the vertex of 'p'.

name: relpoint
documentation: Not yet implemented... <look href = "point(hyperbola, abscissa)"/>

name: relpoint
documentation: Return the relative point of 'co' (0 means origin, 1 means end).

name: angpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'c' in the direction 'x' measured in degrees.

name: angpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'el' in the direction 'x' measured in degrees according to 'polarconicroutine'.

name: angpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'p' in the direction 'x' measured in degrees.

name: angpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'h' in the direction 'x' measured in degrees according to 'polarconicroutine'.

name: curpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'l' which has the curvilinear abscissa 'x'. Origin is l.A.

name: curpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'c' which has the curvilinear abscissa 'x'. Origin is c.center + c.r * (1, 0).

name: curpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'el' which has the curvilinear abscissa 'el'.

name: curpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'p' which has the curvilinear abscissa 'x'. Origin is the vertex of 'p'.

name: curpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'co' which has the curvilinear abscissa 'x'.

name: angabscissa
documentation: Return the angular abscissa of 'M' on the circle 'c'.

name: arc
documentation: Implement oriented ellipse (included circle) arcs. All the calculus with this structure will be as exact as Asymptote can do. For a full precision, you must not cast 'arc' to 'path' excepted for drawing routines. */ ellipse el;/*The support of the arc.*/ restricted real angle0 = 0;/*Internal use: rotating a circle does not modify the origin point,this variable stocks the eventual angle rotation. This value is not used for ellipses which are not circles.*/ restricted real angle1, angle2;/*Values (in degrees) in ]-360, 360[.*/ bool direction = CCW;/*The arc will be drawn from 'angle1' to 'angle2' rotating in the direction 'direction'.*/ polarconicroutine polarconicroutine = currentpolarconicroutine;/*The routine to which the angles refer. If 'el' is a circle 'fromCenter' is always used.

name: setangles
documentation: Set the angles.

name: init
documentation: Constructor.

name: copy
documentation: Copy the arc.

name: polarconicroutine
documentation: Return the default routine used to draw a conic.

name: arc
documentation: Return the ellipse arc from 'angle1' to 'angle2' with respect to 'polarconicroutine' and rotating in the direction 'direction'.

name: complementary
documentation: Return the complementary of 'a'.

name: reverse
documentation: Return arc 'a' oriented in reverse direction.

name: degrees
documentation: Return the measure in degrees of the oriented arc 'a'.

name: angle
documentation: Return the measure in radians of the oriented arc 'a'.

name: arcnodesnumber
documentation: Return the number of nodes to draw the arc 'a'.

name: angpoint
documentation: Return the point given by its angular position (in degrees) relative to the arc 'a'. If 'angle > degrees(a)' or 'angle < 0' the returned point is on the extended arc.

name: draw
documentation: Draw 'arc' adding the pen returned by 'addpenarc(p)' to the pen 'p'. <look href = "#addpenarc"/>

name: arclength
documentation: The arc length of 'a'.

name: point
documentation: Return the point of 'a' which has the abscissa 'l.x' according to the abscissa system 'l.system'. Note that 'a.polarconicroutine' is used instead of 'l.polarconicroutine'. <look href = "#struct abscissa"/>

name: point
documentation: Return the point between node floor(t) and floor(t) + 1.

name: relpoint
documentation: Return the relative point of 'a'. If x > 1 or x < 0, the returned point is on the extended arc.

name: curpoint
documentation: Return the point of 'a' which has the curvilinear abscissa 'x'. If x < 0 or x > arclength(a), the returned point is on the extended arc.

name: angabscissa
documentation: Return the angular abscissa of 'M' according to the arc 'a'.

name: curabscissa
documentation: Return the curvilinear abscissa according to the arc 'a'.

name: nodabscissa
documentation: Return the node abscissa according to the arc 'a'.

name: relabscissa
documentation: Return the relative abscissa according to the arc 'a'.

name: mass
documentation: */ point M;/**/ real m;/*

name: mass
documentation: Constructor of mass point.

name: point
documentation: Cast 'm' to point

name: mass
documentation: Cast 'P' to mass.

name: mass
documentation: Return the mass which has coordinates 'p' with respect to 'R' and weight 'm'.

name: masscenter
documentation: Return the center of the masses 'M'.

name: massformat
documentation: Return the string formated by 'format' with the mass value. In the parameter 'format', %L will be replaced by 's'. <look href = "#defaultmassformat"/>.

name: label
documentation: Draw label returned by massformat(format, L, M) at coordinates of M. <look href = "#massformat(string, string, mass)"/>.

name: dot
documentation: Draw a dot with label 'L' as label(picture, Label, explicit mass, align, string, pen, filltype) does. <look href = "#label(picture, Label, mass, align, string, pen, filltype)"/>.

name: orthocenter
documentation: Return the orthocenter of the triangle ABC.

name: incenter

name: inradius

name: incircle
documentation: Return the incircle of the triangle ABC.

name: excenter
documentation: Return the center of the excircle of the triangle tangent with (AB).

name: exradius
documentation: Return the radius of the excircle of the triangle ABC with (AB).

name: excircle

name: triangle
documentation: */ /**/ struct vertex {/*Structure used to communicate the vertex of a triangle.*/ int n;/*1 means VA,2 means VB,3 means VC,4 means VA etc...*/ triangle t;/*The triangle to which the vertex refers.

name: A,B,C
documentation: The vertices of the triangle (as point).*/ restricted vertex VA, VB, VC;/*The vertices of the triangle (as vertex). Note that the vertex structure contains the triangle to wish it refers.

name: vertex
documentation: Return numbered vertex. 'n' is 1 means VA, 2 means VB, 3 means VC, 4 means VA etc...

name: point
documentation: Return numbered point. n is 1 means A, 2 means B, 3 means C, 4 means A etc...

name: init
documentation: Constructor.

name: operator init
documentation: For backward compatibility. Provide the routine 'triangle(point A, point B, point C)'.

name: init
documentation: For backward compatibility. Provide the routine 'triangle(real b, real alpha, real c, real angle = 0, point A = (0, 0)) which returns the triangle ABC rotated by 'angle' (in degrees) and where b = AC, degrees(A) = alpha, AB = c.

name: a
documentation: Return the length BC. b() and c() are also defined and return the length AC and AB respectively.

name: alpha
documentation: Return the measure (in degrees) of the angle A. beta() and gamma() are also defined and return the measure of the angles B and C respectively.

name: Path
documentation: The path of the triangle.

name: side
documentation: Structure used to communicate the side of a triangle.*/ int n;/*1 or 0 means [AB],-1 means [BA],2 means [BC],-2 means [CB] etc.*/ triangle t;/*The triangle to which the side refers.

name: AB
documentation: For the routines using the structure 'side', triangle.AB means 'side AB'. BA, AC, CA etc are also defined.

name: side
documentation: Return numbered side. n is 1 means AB, -1 means BA, 2 means BC, -2 means CB, etc.

name: line
documentation: Return the numbered line.

name: line
documentation: Return 'side' as line.

name: segment
documentation: Return 'side' as segment.

name: point
documentation: Return the point corresponding to the vertex 'V'.

name: opposite
documentation: Return the opposite side of vertex 'V'.

name: opposite
documentation: Return the opposite vertex of side 'side'.

name: triangleAbc
documentation: Return the triangle ABC rotated by 'angle' with BAC = alpha, AC = b and AB = c.

name: triangleabc
documentation: Return the triangle ABC rotated by 'angle' with BC = a, AC = b and AB = c.

name: triangle
documentation: Return the triangle defined by three line.

name: foot
documentation: Return the endpoint of the altitude from V.

name: foot
documentation: Return the endpoint of the altitude on 'side'.

name: altitude
documentation: Return the altitude passing through 'V'.

name: altitude
documentation: Return the altitude cutting 'side'.

name: orthocenter
documentation: Return the orthocenter of the triangle t.

name: centroid
documentation: Return the centroid of the triangle 't'.

name: circumcenter
documentation: Return the circumcenter of the triangle 't'.

name: circle
documentation: Return the circumcircle of the triangle 't'.

name: circumcircle
documentation: Return the circumcircle of the triangle 't'.

name: incenter
documentation: Return the center of the incircle of the triangle 't'.

name: inradius
documentation: Return the radius of the incircle of the triangle 't'.

name: incircle
documentation: Return the the incircle of the triangle 't'.

name: excenter
documentation: Return the center of the excircle tangent with the side 'side' of its triangle. side = 0 means AB, 1 means AC, other means BC. One must use the predefined sides t.AB, t.AC where 't' is a triangle....

name: exradius
documentation: Return radius of the excircle tangent with the side 'side' of its triangle. side = 0 means AB, 1 means BC, other means CA. One must use the predefined sides t.AB, t.AC where 't' is a triangle....

name: excircle
documentation: Return the excircle tangent with the side 'side' of its triangle. side = 0 means AB, 1 means AC, other means BC. One must use the predefined sides t.AB, t.AC where 't' is a triangle....

name: trilinear
documentation: Trilinear coordinates 'a:b:c' relative to triangle 't'. */ real a,b,c;/*The trilinear coordinates.*/ triangle t;/*The reference triangle.

name: pedal
documentation: Return the pedal triangle of 'M' in 't'. <url href = "http://mathworld.wolfram.com/PedalTriangle.html"/>

name: pedal
documentation: Return the pedal line of 'M' cutting 'side'. <url href = "http://mathworld.wolfram.com/PedalTriangle.html"/>

documentation: <url href = "http://mathworld.wolfram.com/AntipedalTriangle.html"/>

name: extouch
documentation: Return the extouch triangle of the triangle 't'. The extouch triangle of 't' is the triangle formed by the points of tangency of a triangle 't' with its excircles.

name: incentral
documentation: Return the incentral triangle of the triangle 't'. It is the triangle whose vertices are determined by the intersections of the reference triangle's angle bisectors with the respective opposite sides.

name: extouch
documentation: Return the triangle formed by the points of tangency of the triangle referenced by 'side' with its excircles. One vertex of the returned triangle is on the segment 'side'.

name: bisectorpoint
documentation: The intersection point of the angle bisector from the opposite point of 'side' with the side 'side'.

name: bisector
documentation: Return the interior bisector passing through 'V' rotated by angle (in degrees) around 'V'.

name: bisector
documentation: Return the bisector of the line segment 'side'.

name: intouch
documentation: The point of tangency on the side 'side' of its incircle.

name: intouch
documentation: Return the intouch triangle of the triangle 't'. The intouch triangle of 't' is the triangle formed by the points of tangency of a triangle 't' with its incircles.

name: tangential
documentation: Return the tangential triangle of the triangle 't'. The tangential triangle of 't' is the triangle formed by the lines tangent to the circumcircle of the given triangle 't' at its vertices.

name: medial
documentation: Return the triangle whose vertices are midpoints of the sides of 't'.

name: median
documentation: Return median from 'V'.

name: median
documentation: Return median from the opposite vertex of 'side'.

name: orthic
documentation: Return the triangle whose vertices are endpoints of the altitudes from each of the vertices of 't'.

name: symmedial
documentation: Return the symmedial triangle of 't'.

name: anticomplementary
documentation: Return the triangle which has the given triangle 't' as its medial triangle.

name: intersectionpoints
documentation: Return the intersection points. If 'extended' is true, the sides are lines else the sides are segments. intersectionpoints(line, triangle, bool) is also defined.

name: dir
documentation: The direction (towards the outside of the triangle) of the interior angle bisector of 'V'.

name: lvoid label
documentation: Draw 'L' on picture 'pic' at vertex 'V' aligned by 'alignFactor * align'.

name: label
documentation: Draw labels LA, LB and LC aligned in the rotated (by 'alignAngle' in degrees) direction (towards the outside of the triangle) of the interior angle bisector of vertices. One can individually modify the alignment by setting the Label parameter 'align'.

name: show
documentation: Draw triangle and labels of sides and vertices.

name: draw
documentation: Draw sides of the triangle 't' on picture 'pic' using pen 'p'.

name: draw
documentation: Draw sides of the triangles 't' on picture 'pic' using pen 'p'.

name: drawline
documentation: Draw lines of the triangle 't' on picture 'pic' using pen 'p'.

name: dot
documentation: Draw a dot at each vertex of 't'.

documentation: Return the tangent of 'c' at the intersection point of the half-line'[c.C M)'.

name: point
documentation: Return the intersection point of 'c' with the half-line '[c.C v)'.

name: tangent
documentation: Return the tangent of 'c' at the point M so that vec(c.C M) is collinear to 'v' with the same sense.

name: tangent
documentation: Return the tangent of 'el' at 'point(el, x)'.

name: tangents
documentation: Return the tangents of 'el' passing through 'M'.

name: tangent
documentation: Return the tangent of 'p' at 'point(p, x)' (use the Wells method).

name: tangents
documentation: Return the tangent of 'p' at 'M' (use the Wells method).

name: tangent
documentation: Return the tangent of 'h' at 'point(p, x)'.

name: tangents
documentation: Return the tangent of 'h' at 'M'.

name: intersectionpoints
documentation: intersectionpoints(arc, circle) is also defined.

name: intersectionpoints
documentation: intersectionpoints(arc, line) is also defined.

name: arcsubtendedcenter
documentation: Return the center of the arc retuned by the 'arcsubtended' routine.

name: arcsubtended
documentation: Return the arc circle from which the segment AB is saw with the angle 'angle'. If the point 'M' is on this arc, the oriented angle (MA, MB) is equal to 'angle'.

name: arccircle
documentation: Return the CCW arc circle 'AB' passing through 'M'.

name: arc
documentation: Return the arc from 'point(c, x1)' to 'point(c, x2)' in the direction 'direction'.

name: arc
documentation: Return the arc from 'M' to 'N' in the direction 'direction'. The points 'M' and 'N' must belong to the ellipse 'el'.

name: arccircle
documentation: Return the arc circle centered on A from B to rotate(angle, A) * B in the direction 'direction'.

name: arc
documentation: Return the arc from 'point(a, x1)' to 'point(a, x2)' traversed in the direction of the arc direction.

name: arc
documentation: Return the arc from 'M' to 'N'. The points 'M' and 'N' must belong to the arc 'a'.

name: inverse
documentation: Return the inverse arc circle of 's' with respect to inversion 'i'.

name: compassmark
documentation: Return an arc centered on O with the angle 'angle' so that the position of 'A' on this arc makes an angle 'position * angle'.

name: tangent
documentation: Return the tangent of 'a' at 'point(a, x)'.

name: tangent
documentation: Return the tangent of 'a' at 'M'. The points 'M' must belong to the arc 'a'.
